classes.description: An application's top level scripting object.
classes.name: application

properties.description: Auto terminate the application when all windows are closed?
properties.name: auto terminate

classes.description: A virtual machine registered in UTM.
classes.name: virtual machine

properties.description: The unique identifier of the VM.
properties.name: id

classes.description: A serial port in the guest that can be connected to from the host.
classes.name: serial port

properties.description: The unique identifier of the tag.
properties.name: id

properties.description: The type of serial interface on the host.
properties.name: interface

properties.description: Host address of the serial port (determined by the interface type).
properties.name: address

properties.description: Port number of the serial port (not used in some interface types).
properties.name: port

commands.description: Create a new virtual machine.
commands.name: make

parameters.description: Specify 'virtual machine' here.
parameters.name: new

parameters.description: You must specify the backend as well as a configuration with a name. If this is a QEMU virtual machine, you must specify the architecture in the configuration as well.
parameters.name: with properties

commands.description: Start a virtual machine or resume a suspended virtual machine.
commands.name: start

parameters.description: When false, do not save the VM changes to disk. Default value is true.
parameters.name: saving

commands.description: Suspend a running virtual machine to memory.
commands.name: suspend

parameters.description: Save VM state to disk after suspend. Default value is false.
parameters.name: saving

commands.description: Shuts down a running virtual machine.
commands.name: stop

parameters.description: Method to stop the VM.
parameters.name: by

commands.description: Delete a virtual machine. All data will be deleted, there is no confirmation!
commands.name: delete

commands.description: Copy an virtual machine and all its data.
commands.name: duplicate

parameters.description: Only the configuration can be changed.
parameters.name: with properties

commands.description: Import a new virtual machine from a file.
commands.name: import

parameters.description: Specify 'virtual machine' here.
parameters.name: new

parameters.description: The virtual machine file (.utm) to import.
parameters.name: from

commands.description: Export a virtual machine to a specified location.
commands.name: export

parameters.description: Location to export the VM to.
parameters.name: to

values.description: Apple Virtualization.framework backend.
values.name: apple

values.description: QEMU backend.
values.name: qemu

values.description: The virtual machine is not currently available.
values.name: unavailable

values.description: VM is not running.
values.name: stopped

values.description: VM is starting up.
values.name: starting

values.description: VM is running.
values.name: started

values.description: VM is going to pause.
values.name: pausing

values.description: VM is paused.
values.name: paused

values.description: VM is resuming from pause.
values.name: resuming

values.description: VM is stopping.
values.name: stopping

values.description: Force stop VM by sending stop request to the backend.
values.name: force

values.description: Force kill VM by terminating the backend.
values.name: kill

values.description: Send a power down request to the guest OS which may be ignored.
values.name: request

values.description: Pseudo TTY port.
values.name: ptty

values.description: TCP port.
values.name: tcp

values.description: Serial interface is currently unavailable or is in use by the GUI.
values.name: unavailable

classes.description: A file that resides on the guest.
classes.name: guest file

commands.description: Open a file on the guest. You must close the file when you are done to prevent leaking guest resources.
commands.name: open file

parameters.description: The guest path of the file to open.
parameters.name: at

parameters.description: Open mode.
parameters.name: for

parameters.description: If true, will open for both reading and writing. The file existance requirement and creation is still governed by the open mode. Default is false.
parameters.name: updating

commands.description: Execute a command or script on the guest.
commands.name: execute

parameters.description: Either the full path of the executable to run or an executable found in the guest's PATH environment.
parameters.name: at

parameters.description: List of arguments to pass to the executable.
parameters.name: with arguments

parameters.description: List of environment variables to pass to the executable. Each entry should be in the format NAME=VALUE.
parameters.name: with environment

parameters.description: Data to feed into the process's standard input. If using base64 encoding, this should be a valid base64 string.
parameters.name: using input

parameters.description: Input data is base64 encoded. The data will be decoded before being passed to the executable. Default is false.
parameters.name: base64 encoding

parameters.description: If true, the standard output and error will be captured and accessible in the returned object. You need to call update on the object to get the data. Default is false.
parameters.name: output capturing

commands.description: Query the guest for all IP addresses on its network interfaces (excluding loopback).
commands.name: query ip

commands.description: Reads text data from a guest file.
commands.name: read

parameters.description: Specify the offset to start reading from. Default value is zero.
parameters.name: at offset

parameters.description: Specify where the offset is from. Default value is from the current file pointer.
parameters.name: from

parameters.description: Amount of bytes to read. The limit is 48 MB. Default is to read until the end.
parameters.name: for length

parameters.description: If true, then the result will be base64 encoded. This is recommended if you are reading a binary file. Default is false.
parameters.name: base64 encoding

parameters.description: If true, the file will be closed after reading and must be opened again to perform more operations. If false, you can perform multiple reads on the same open file. The default is true.
parameters.name: closing

commands.description: Pulls a file from the guest to the host.
commands.name: pull

parameters.description: The host file in which to save the guest file.
parameters.name: to

parameters.description: If true, the file will be closed after reading and must be opened again to perform more operations. If false, you can perform multiple reads on the same open file. The default is true.
parameters.name: closing

commands.description: Writes text data to a guest file.
commands.name: write

parameters.description: Data to write to the guest file. If base64 encoding is specified, this should be a valid base64 string which will be decoded before writing.
parameters.name: with data

parameters.description: Specify the offset to start writing to. Default value is zero.
parameters.name: at offset

parameters.description: Specify where the offset is from. Default value is from the current file pointer.
parameters.name: from

parameters.description: If true, then the input data is base64 encoded. This is recommended if you are writing a binary file. Default is false.
parameters.name: base64 encoding

parameters.description: If true, the file will be closed after writing and must be opened again to perform more operations. If false, you can perform multiple reads on the same open file. The default is true.
parameters.name: closing

commands.description: Pushes a file from the host to the guest and closes it.
commands.name: push

parameters.description: The host file in which to send to the guest.
parameters.name: from

parameters.description: If true, the file will be closed after writing and must be opened again to perform more operations. If false, you can perform multiple reads on the same open file. The default is true.
parameters.name: closing

commands.description: Closes the file and prevent further operations.
commands.name: close

commands.description: Fetch execution result from the guest.

direct_parameter.description: Guest process to fetch result from.

commands.name: get result

values.description: Open the file as read only. The file must exist.
values.name: reading

values.description: Open the file for writing. If the file does not exist, it will be created. If the file exists, it will be overwritten.
values.name: writing

values.description: Open the file for writing at the end. Offsets are ignored for writes. If the file does not exist, it will be created.
values.name: appending

values.description: The start of the file (only positive offsets).
values.name: start position

values.description: The current pointer (both positive and negative offsets).
values.name: current position

values.description: The end of the file (only negative offsets for reads, both for writes).
values.name: end position

properties.description: The configuration of the virtual machine.
properties.name: configuration

commands.description: Update the configuration of the virtual machine. The VM must be in the stopped state.
commands.name: update configuration

parameters.description: The configuration to update the virtual machine. You cannot change the backend with this!
parameters.name: with

values.description: Do not enable directory sharing.
values.name: none

values.description: Use SPICE WebDav (SPICE guest tools required).
values.name: WebDAV

values.description: Use VirtFS mount tagged 'share' (VirtFS guest drivers required).
values.name: VirtFS

values.description: Emulate a VLAN.
values.name: emulated

values.description: NAT based sharing with the host.
values.name: shared

values.description: NAT based sharing with no WAN routing.
values.name: host

values.description: Bridged to a host interface.
values.name: bridged

values.description: NAT based sharing with the host.
values.name: shared

values.description: Bridged to a host interface.
values.name: bridged

classes.description: An application's top level scripting object.
classes.name: application

classes.description: A host USB device that is shared with the guest.
classes.name: usb device

properties.description: A unique identifier corrosponding to the USB bus and port number.
properties.name: id

properties.description: The name of the USB device.
properties.name: name

properties.description: The product name described by the iManufacturer descriptor.
properties.name: manufacturer name

properties.description: The product name described by the iProduct descriptor.
properties.name: product name

properties.description: The vendor ID described by the idVendor descriptor.
properties.name: vendor id

properties.description: The product ID described by the idProduct descriptor.
properties.name: product id

commands.description: Connect a USB device to a running VM and remove it from the host.

direct_parameter.description: The USB device to connect to the VM.

commands.name: connect

parameters.description: The virtual machine to connect. The virtual machine must be running. Not all backends support USB sharing.
parameters.name: to

commands.description: Disconnect a USB device from the guest and re-assign it to the host.
commands.name: disconnect

properties.description: The registry of the virtual machine.
properties.name: registry

commands.description: Update the registry of the virtual machine.
commands.name: update registry

parameters.description: The registry to update the virtual machine. Currently you can only change the shared directory with this!
parameters.name: with
